patch.value: GH-427: Windows Node 24 nightly fails — POSIX-only error-code assertions (EACCES/EISDIR) in restore-command and history-rewrite specs

Nightly Validation run 28698219516 (2026-07-04, windows-latest/Node 24): 4 test failures. restore-command.spec asserts rejects.toMatchObject({code:'EACCES'}) after chmod 0o444 on the history file, but Windows Node 24 raises EPERM when opening a read-only file for append. history-rewrite.spec asserts EISDIR when writing to a directory path; Windows raises EPERM. Fix: platform-tolerant assertions (EACCES|EPERM, EISDIR|EPERM).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Fix applied (uncommitted): restore-command.spec 3x rejects.toMatchObject code now expect.stringMatching(/^(EACCES|EPERM)$/); history-rewrite.spec EISDIR assertion now toMatch(/EISDIR|EPERM/). Both spec files pass locally (42 tests). Root cause: Windows Node 24 fs raises EPERM where POSIX raises EACCES (read-only append) / EISDIR (write to dir path).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: 2026-07-04 verification: platform-tolerant assertions pass in the full fast suite (5336/5336) on Linux; the EACCES|EPERM / EISDIR|EPERM alternations keep POSIX coverage while accepting Windows Node 24 EPERM. Windows confirmation will come from the next nightly after push (GH-427 stays open until then).

value.note: Windows Node 24 EPERM-compatible directory write assertion

GH-427 still needs confirmation from GitHub Windows Node 24 CI/nightly after push.

value.text: Platform-tolerant assertions (EACCES|EPERM, EISDIR|EPERM) landed on main via PR #428 head. Remaining: confirm next windows-latest Node 24 nightly is green, then close this item and GH-427.

patch.value: windows-latest Node 24 nightly is green with platform-tolerant error-code assertions; GH-427 closed.

## Context
Tracks GH-427. Nightly Validation run 28698219516 (2026-07-04, windows-latest / Node 24): 4 failures, all POSIX-only error-code assertions.

- restore-command.spec: expects code EACCES after chmod 0o444 on the history file; Windows Node 24 raises EPERM opening a read-only file for append.
- history-rewrite.spec: expects EISDIR writing to a directory path; Windows raises EPERM.

## Approach
Platform-tolerant assertions: accept EACCES|EPERM and EISDIR|EPERM respectively (match on a set, not equality). Precedent: PR#365 Windows fixes and the 2026-07-04 GH-427 EPERM fix noted in the ecosystem audit — check whether that partial fix already covers some of the 4 and only patch the remainder. The windows-regression CI job is the oracle; nightly re-run green closes GH-427.

## Definition of done
All 4 specs pass on windows-latest Node 24 nightly; no weakening on POSIX (still asserts the specific POSIX code there or the set matcher documents why); GH-427 closed with the green run linked.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Evidence: local full coverage and lint passed after Windows-tolerant assertions and lock/claim changes. GitHub issue #427 remains open because the latest Nightly Validation run is still the 2026-07-04 failure and no newer nightly has validated the fix yet.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Evidence update: current branch full coverage and pnpm lint are green after review fixes. GH-427 remains open until the pushed PR head or next nightly confirms windows-latest Node 24; do not close from local Linux evidence alone.
